steps.title: Place however inside the clause
steps.content: The sentence first states that the designation is hypothetical and then explains why: Proto-Cariban is only postulated. The transition however is being set off within the first clause, and the following independent clause needs stronger punctuation before it.

steps.title: Use the needed separator
steps.content:
• Choice A: A period changes the intended one-sentence construction and moves however to the next clause.
  • Choice C: A colon before however is not the standard way to set off the transition.
  • Choice D: A comma after however creates a comma splice between two independent clauses.
The answer is B.
